english: Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the Module-3M armour, and make skier proud
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the PACA, the strongest armour in all the lands (Twitch variant allowed)
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the 6B2 armour, and then never touch it again
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the Kora-Kulon armour, and ask why a light class 3 armour is 11.1 kg
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the UNTAR armour, you blueberry you
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the Zhuk-3 armour, and embrace your role as a member of the press
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the *REDACTED* armour (not gonna force you to wear it, just kill 3 pmcs in general)
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the Kirasa-N armour, and realize how overpriced it is
Kill 5 PMCs while wearing the Trooper armour, extra because everyone uses it
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the THOR CRV armour, because concealable armour is very useful here
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the 6B13 armour, and watch your armour disintegrate in 3 hits
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the 6B23-2 armour, and blend in with all the desert in Tarkov
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the OTV armour, and ponder why BSG keeps adding light, class 4 ceramic armour
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the HPC armour, I have nothing funny to say it's actually not bad
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the Killa armour, and question how this is light armour
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the GHZEL-K armour, and don't ask why scavs can have it
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the Hexgrid armour, and wonder why a class 6 is light
Kill 3 PMCs while wearing the Zhuk-6a armour, and continue wondering why a class 6 is light
Kill Killa once because I said so
...uh, GL? You good?
Death.
...heh.
Kill.
It has a point, I really should be taking care of them.

Taking care of your weapons is by far one of the best things you can do to improve your chances of survival in Tarkov. They're what's keeping you alive, so you should keep them alive, too! To do this, though, you'll need the right tools. I'm sure there's some sort of repair kit out there, right? Normally I wouldn't fuss about whether it's brand new or 'found in raid' or whatever, but this is important enough that you should find, or craft, it yourself.
Kitting out
Yeah, that looks right! Before we use it, though, I think I should show you why it's so important.
'Hand over' a FIR Weapon Repair Kit
Hand in an AR or SMG with <2 durability remaining
This is going to be interesting

Have you ever used a weapon for so long that it barely works anymore? It jams, it breaks down, it misfires, it just shouldn't be relied upon for combat. Sometimes, studying such a weapon can be of great value for understanding future weapon repairs, as well as being a grim reminder of what can happen if you don't take proper care of your firearm. Try wearing a weapon down to scrap. You might want to bring another weapon in while you do this...
Ol' (un)reliable
Wow, look at that thing. Do you even want it back? Well, I can't exactly take it... just go leave it by your workbench, I guess.
I should deconstruct some weapons at the workbench for this.

Have you ever seen a disassembled weapon before? I sure hope you have, given that you are a trained military guy and all that. Being able to identify various weapon parts is critical to being able to effectively repair your weapon. Try taking some weapons apart, or even better, try finding some new ones in raid. Buy a VPO-209 from Skier and deconstruct it at your workbench, see what you get.
Reduced to the base parts
Look at all that metal! I think you've seen enough scrap and oil to know exactly what to look out for when repairing your weapon, so let's go do it.
'Hand over' 15 FIR weapon parts
Seems... easy? Is there a catch? Ehhh, some calibers aren't the easiest to get, I suppose.

You know what we haven't really done yet? Practied with all of the different weapons. 'But light, why don't we use weapon repair kits?' Because it doesn't work. So instead, you're gonna learn about the different calibers of weapons. I want you to grab some kills with every caliber you can find. I'll spare you the grenade launchers, at least.
Omnicaliber
Got a feel for each one, have ya? Can you tell their strenghts and weaknesses? Ah well, the next one should be enjoyable.
Kill 5 enemies with 20/70 (TOZ is the only one)
Kill 5 enemies with 12/70
Kill 5 enemies with 23x75mm (KS-23 is the only one)
Kill 5 enemies with 7.62x25mm Tokarev
Kill 5 enemies with 9x18mm Makarov
Kill 5 enemies with 9x19mm Parabellum
Kill 5 enemies with 9x21mm Gyurza
Kill 5 enemies with .357 Magnum
Kill 5 enemies with .45 ACP
Kill 5 enemies with 4.6x30mm HK
Kill 5 enemies with 5.7x28mm FN
Kill 5 enemies with 5.45x39mm
Kill 5 enemies with 5.56x45mm NATO
Kill 5 enemies with 6.8x51mm (MCX SPEAR is the only one)
Kill 5 enemies with .300 Blackout (MCX is the only one)
Kill 5 enemies with 7.62x39mm
Kill 5 enemies with 7.62x51mm NATO
Kill 5 enemies with 7.62x54mmR
Kill 5 enemies with .338 Lapua Magnum
Kill 5 enemies with 9x39mm
Kill 5 enemies with .366 TKM
Kill 5 enemies with 12.7x55mm STs-130
Kill 5 enemies with .50 Action Express
Sure hope 10 is enough

Say what you will about Tarkov, this is real life we're talking about. Your magazines don't magically combine like the video games you grew up on. Here, you gotta take care of them. That means you gotta get good at loading, unloading, and switching mags fast. We'll start off with the lil 10 rounders. Let's do one kill for each bullet you can hold.
Starting small

Less is more, they say. If you have a 10 round magazine, it's lighter, likely more ergonomic, and most importantly will cause you to think more carefully about your shots. In that way, a 10 round magazine can be way more effective than a larger one.
Kill 10 enemies while using 10 round mags
Upgrades.

The 10 rounders were nice, but sometimes that's just not enough ammo to seal the deal. Let's double the amount of bullets we can use, how about that? Still low enough that you'll be a little more careful, but plenty incase you miss a few or if the enemy is heavily armoured.
Upgrades, people
So, do you prefer the 10 or the 20 so far? Perhaps the 20 allows different guns that didn't have access to 10 rounds.
Kill 20 enemies while using 20 round mags
More killing, less complaining

Alright, now let's get used to what you're most commonly be working with. Normally I'd stick with the whole 'one kill per capacity' thing, but 30 round magazines are standard issue, so I think it's the most important capacity to be familiar with. How about you double what you'd normally do?
Standard Issue
Was the extra work so bad? I told you, 30 round mags are everywhere. So many guns can use them.
Kill 60 enemies while using 30 round mags
P90 gang

How about something more fun? 50 rounders aren't as common, but you can still find plenty of weapons that use them. More bullets means you lose out on the 'bonuses' the smaller mags give you, but more bullets! Who's going to complain about more bullets?
50 dead men
You have killed 120 people for this training so far. You'd think this is a combat taskline, at this rate.
Kill 50 enemies while using 50 round mags
To be fair, 60 is quite a lot

Hot take: If you need 60 round magazines, you either found yourself in a REALLY bad situation, or you need to work on your aim. Oh well, they exist, and sometimes you just really need to suppress a location. Don't waste all your ammo, though! That stuff can be expensive.
Overkill
Mag drills complete! You should have no issue transferring these skills to the more unusual magazine capacities, I believe in you.
Kill 60 enemies while using 60 round mags
Wait, how are we going to train this...?

Tarkov, being real life, is a rather unique place. It turns out that getting shot has consequences. Who'dve thought, right? If you're going to get shot, it's going to mess you up, and you need to know how to get yourself back in working order. First, we need the right tools. Go get some surgical kits, a few of each.
Preparing for the worst
Some of the red, some of the black... We'll be using most of these, don't worry. That's not what you're worried about? Ah, you can't die, you'll be fine
Acquire 5 CMS kits
Acquire 3 SURV-12 kits
I suppose it's useful, but I wish we could do this in a way that didn't involve me getting shot a lot?
Now that you have the tools, let's get to work. I'm afraid there's only going to be one person you can practice on... erm... do what you must.
Field practice
Yeah, you're getting the hang of it! Let's keep working, there's plenty more to practice.
Use a CMS kit 5 times
Use a SURV-12 kit 5 times
Why are we writing quests where I have to take damage? What sadist is causing this?

Sometimes, you just get shot a lot, yet your suffering doesn't end. I've seen people healing for minutes around here. Granted, you should be thankful that healing from multiple gunshot wounds is just a couple minutes, there's something not right about that, but whatever. Anyways, try doing surgery multiple times in one raid.
Preemptive measures
Good! Knowing how to do surgery is good, but not needing to do surgery is even better. Try to keep it that way, eh?
Regain 3000 health
That is... technically a slang definition of surgical, I guess. Oh well, beats jumping off the roof 50 times.

You know, so far these tasks have been sort of... out of your control, I guess? Like, you could just be jumping off buildings to do this, but I don't imagine you're doing that very often, that's no fun. So, how about a different kind of surgical? It's technically cheating, but hey, I really can only think of so many ways to make training surgery interesting. We'll get back to the grind next task, but let's have some fun for a bit, ok? I want 8 headshots, with bolt-actions, in the same raid. That's quite surgical, eh?
A different kind of 'surgical'
Good shooting, m8. Back to the less fun stuff, sadly.
Kill 8 enemies with headshots with bolt-action rifles in a single raid.
That's a lot of roofs to jump off of, I guess. (This is not actually the intended way to complete this, please don't do that)

I hope last task was a little more enjoyable, a nice break from what we're doing. Unfortunately, there's really only one way I can actually train you on your surgery... and that's to do surgery. So, being the final task, I really have no choice but to do it that way. I highly recommend letting this one happen naturally, it will over time. You're not missing out by not completing your training, if you think about it. If you don't need to be performing surgery, you don't need to be an expert in surgery. It's up to you if you even want to go for it, honestly.
Back to the grind

Well, that's done now. You can probably be a surgeon now, once you get out of here. Also, you moved you liver into the wrong place when repairing your stomach that one time. I wonder if that'll fix itself or not when you die again? We'll fine out.
Perform surgery a total of 60 times
At least 10 with the CMS
At least 15 with the SURV-12
QUESTS WITH *** HAVE MULTIPLE CHOICES. CHOOSE WISELY.
Kill 15 pmcs with either AA-12
Kill 3 PMCs with the V40 Mini
If only I could find a Ray Gun in one of these boxes...

Searching seems like a valuable skill to have here. You're still searching for an answer to some things right now, aren't you? Sorry, sorry. Well... Hmm. Let's start with the basics. Go open some containers and see what's inside. Make sure you move everything around a bunch and make a ton of noise like a trained military soldier would (BSG why is looting so loud~)
Mystery Box
Good, glad to see you know how to open a lid. Or a backpack. Or a drawer. Or a - whatever, you know what you're doing.
Pickup 40 items
Search 20 containers
ugh.
If tasked with searching one specific area in painstaking detail, how would you fare? Could you find the needle in the haystack? Or will the lack of dopamine burn you out? Let's see.
Thorough Investigation
So you can dedicate your time to such a task, if needed. You will find this to be a valuable skill indeed.
Search 60 containers in one raid
Aren't there enough quests about running around the whole place? Yeesh.

Something isn't right here...

After visiting Ground Zero on two separate occasions, you've noticed that both raids felt... similar. In fact, it was as if the first raid never happened; none of the fresh bodies were there, but previous deaths remained right where they were. The container you were sure you searched just hours ago somehow had items in it again. Either someone is running an extremely sick social experiment, or you're suffering from hallucinations.
Suspicion

Again, it was as if the previous raid never happened... Even the blood from those you shot last time wasn't there. Is this all a dream or something? What the hell is going on? ...You faintly hear a high-pitched voice in your head, but cannot understand what it's saying.

english: ***Which will you do?***
Seek.
Rest.
Know.
Find.
QUESTS WITH *** HAVE MULTIPLE CHOICES. CHOOSE WISELY.
There has to be something I can discover...

'Seek' was the first word you heard, so that must be what's most important, right? It makes sense, you reason. There must be something that needs to be found. A switch, a mastermind, an escape. Whatever it is, you can find it if you look hard enough.
Seek

You search, and search, and search some more for anything that could provide the broadest explanation. Nothing. There are items, sure. Things that will help you in the short term. Yet your search bears no fruit... until a familiar voice asks, 'Are you seeking me?'
There has to be something I can locate...

Finding is what you need to do, right? Seeking never ends, but finding provides an endpoint, a tangible result. Though, what are you trying to find...? You'll know when you find it, surely. You'll hear that voice again; maybe a director will say 'CUT!' and reveal it's all a big game. ...Maybe.
Find

Nothing you find seems to be important to this madness. Things that might aid you in this purgatory, sure, but as far as the broader picture goes, you're no better off. You can't seem to find the right answer... That is, until a familiar voice playfully says, 'Found you.'
There has to be something I can think of...

You've been searching for quite a while, and haven't found anything useful... Yet, how are you supposed to 'know' if you can't understand what's going on? You need to increase your awareness of things...
Know

With some practice, you are able to increase your mental capacities a bit... While you still can't find anything useful within Tarkov, you notice a sense within you... a feeling that, maybe, what's going on here is beyond some mere 'thing'. As soon as you think that, you hear the voice again: 'Now you're getting somewhere.'
Maybe I just need a break...

You're too stressed out. Whatever's going on is far too much, and maybe the first step in solving this is to understand that. You decide it might be best to take a bit of a break, clear your head. You pace about your hideout, unable to fully cease the thinking but able to clear your head somewhat. You're not crazy, you're not hallucinating... right? You need something to take the edge off.
Rest

'Are you beginning to lose yourself?' A calm voice asks. Though, the sudden address from an invisible source does nothing to actually calm you down. 'Woah, didn't mean to scare ya. Though, I suppose its hard not to, when some voice just starts talking to you. Sorry about that.' The voice pauses.
Take a smoke break. (Hand over any cigarettes or lighter)
Loot 50 items
Search 30 containers
This is not what I was expecting, to say the least.

'You spent so long searching, but couldn't see what was right there? Yet you haven't died, so you must be fairly perceptive... or lucky. Either way, you needn't seek any longer, for I am who you sought. Allow me to introduce myself properly: I am an angel of Heaven, most often called Guiding Light. My work takes place in an unseen realm, though its effects are experienced by all, whether they realize it or not. I never speak directly to people, though for you, an exception needs to be made... well, let me not get ahead of myself. I should explain as much as I can to you.\u2019 The voice fades. It seems to be waiting for you to get ready for something, perhaps whatever story it is about to tell.'
Sought After
'You lit a fire just for me?' The voice sounds genuinely flattered. 'Well, that\u2019s\u2026 awfully nice of you. I\u2019ll start right away'
This is not what I was expecting, to say the least.

'I was wondering when you'd begin to notice, or if you would ever notice. I'm proud of you. Allow me to explain some things. I am a celestial being, named Guiding Light. I perform my work on a realm you can't sense with your mind, though there are some that use something else to see it. Everyone has what they need already with them, so you were wise to look inward... hmm. I wonder, can you find it now?\u2019 The voice fades. It seems to be waiting for you to do something.'
Known

'Hah! He does often appear in fire, doesn\u2019t he?' While the voice has previously come from within your mind, this time it seems to speak from within the fire you lit. 'You might know yet. We\u2019ll see over time. For now, I have a lot to share with you.'
Reach level 2 on either of the following skills:
Attention
Perception
What choice do I really have?

You decide to return to this voice, whatever that even means. You can't really *return* to something you don't know... but seconds after you make your decision, you hear it again. 'Welcome back'. It speaks not in a sarcastic tone, but a genuine, inviting one, as if a child has returned home to its parent.
Return
(See next quest)
Continue
If I choose what I do, but someone already knows what I choose... How does that work??

'Where do I begin... well, you know the tale of Tarkov better than most, since your company sent you here. You know about TerraGroup, you know the conflict, you probably know the result to some extent... but I doubt you know much about the cultists here. You see, the combination of TerraGroup's misdeeds and the activity of the cult have... done something. We know that the Father knows; He knows everything. And all things are accounted for in His plan, so whatever is happening is allowed to be happening. ...That being said, we don't take lightly to someone creating whatever this has become. Disrupting the natural flow of life and death... Ah, yes, I should probably mention that. There is no death here anymore. What you kill comes back, and when someone eventually kills you, you'll come back too. Fun, right? Now, someone like me having such blatant and sustained contact with one of His children is abnormal... but, Tarkov is quite abnormal at the moment. I was instructed to do this, anyways. To provide you with some guidance. What you choose to do from here, whether it's aligned with my assistance or not, is up to you. It always has been, and always will be your choice. For whatever you choose is foreknown by Him and Him alone, and we trust that He has accounted for it, for He has. So while I hope you choose to listen to me, just know that really, it is up to you. You benefit, after all. ...Alright, I've talked your ears out by now. Enjoy a bit of a rest; that's how all of man's labor should start. I will contact you again soon. Farewell, friend.'
A Story by the Fire
You feel the presence fade. Something tells you it should return eventually.
'Thank you for listening'

This... this is an interesting situation.

You call out to the voice, however that even works. Can you call out to something you aren't sure of? Apparently, yes, because you get a response. 'Hello, dear traveler. You seem lost. What brings you to a place like this?' You question whether to answer it, but it continues after a few seconds. 'Well, whatever the reason, you are here now. You can't seem to leave either; even death couldn't free you. Yes, I watched you die. I've seen all that you've experienced here; but do not be afraid, for I am an angel of Heaven. Please, call me Guiding Light. I want to help you. May I?' The voice ceases after the question.
***Partial Explanation***
***Your choice is made***
'May I?'
Yes
No
What am I getting myself into...
Thank you. I think I should start with an explanation of some things... hmm. Would you light a little fire for us? It's a bit of a long story...
A Little Assistance
'Perfect.' (See next quest)
I just don't know how I feel about this voice...
'Oh. Well... alright then. ...Uhm, if you change your mind, let me know.' You sense a presence fade from you. You find yourself free to do what you want. ...As long as it's within Tarkov, of course.
***Decline***
***Your choice is made***
Change your mind, and let the voice help you.

Does it get tiring holding that gun up all the time? Espeically if you're waiting for the other person to come to you, you could run out of stamina at the wrong time... you should try bracing your weapon against something. A wall, a box, a bipod, a ledge, there's a lot of places you can set up. Helps with the recoil too! Go give that a try.

english: I'm... alive?

During your attempt to discern an answer, you met an unfortunate end... ...or, didn't you? Then why are you still here? You wonder. This can't have been just a nightmare, right? Though it would explain the voice you heard... No, everything looks just the same; it can't have been a dream... You conclude, in your confusion, that your only option is to carry on... though you swear you can hear the voice again, this time with no understandable words. Despite that, you get the sense that you need to venture out again.
Cut Short

The voice grows louder each raid, growing from almost nothing to a whisper, to a murmur, to a soft voice. It suddenly speaks to you with clear, but duplicated words: 'You've run around quite enough. You are tired, confused, lost... Rest, and come to me.' With that, the voice fades out.
Complete 3 raids
I'm going crazy...
finish_summary: Quest[target=683c03613064d9810dfbe0fe; status=2] || CounterCreator[kills(target=Any,value=1,distance=5555,daytime=0-0)] || Quest[target=68452e81ed96a8107bd8763a; status=4] || Quest[target=68452e81ed96a8107bd8763a; status=4] || Quest[target=68452e81ed96a8107bd8763a; status=4]

english: You recall what the voice said... 'Rest, and come to me.' You don't really know how you can 'go' to a voice you can't identify, but perhaps you can call out to it...? You can hear it, so maybe it will hear you. It sounded like two voices merged together though... is it one entity or two? You can't tell... You get the sense that you can't put this decision off any longer. Even if they're just hallucinations, would listening to them even hurt anymore?

english: QUESTS WITH *** HAVE MULTIPLE CHOICES. CHOOSE WISELY.
I seem to be left without a choice

You die again... yet find yourself back at your hideout once more. If the first death wasn't confirmation, this has to be; death has no operation in Tarkov, or at least, you seem to fall out of its clutches. You haven't found a way out of this place yet, either... ...the voice have only grown louder, almost becoming understandable. It sounds echoed. Perhaps it's time to confront it, if that's even possible.
Death is No Escape
It suddenly speaks to you with clear, but duplicated words: 'You've run around quite enough. You are tired, confused, lost... Rest, and come to me.' With that, the voice fades out.
Casually chatting with an otherwordly being

'Like I've said, you're doing pretty well for yourself, all things considered. You've spent a considerable amount of time here, yet have only died once. ...Ah, yes, I should probably touch on that matter. For starters, let me introduce myself. I am an angel of Heaven, named 'Guiding Light'. I've been watching over Tarkov since before you were sent here, and have seen quite a lot of what has happened. It... well, hah, this will be a long story. How about you light a little fire to sit by while I rant a bit?'
Conversation with an Otherwordly Being(1)
'Perfect''. (See next quest)
Call out to the voices
Hand over any flammable material
If I choose what I do, but someone already knows what I choose... How does that work??

'Where do I begin... well, you know the tale of Tarkov better than most, since your company sent you here. You know about TerraGroup, you know the conflict, you probably know the result to some extent... but I doubt you know much about the cultists here. You see, the combination of TerraGroup's misdeeds and the activity of the cult have... done something. We know that the Father knows; He knows everything. And all things are accounted for in His plan, so whatever is happening is allowed to be happening. ...That being said, we don't take lightly to someone creating whatever this has become. Disrupting the natural flow of life and death... Well, you've experienced this already. There is no death here anymore. What you kill comes back, and when you die again, you'll come back too. Fun, right? Now, someone like me having such blatant and sustained contact with one of His children is abnormal... but, Tarkov is quite abnormal at the moment. I was instructed to do this, anyway. To provide you with some guidance. What you choose to do from here, whether it's aligned with my assistance or not, is up to you. It always has been, and always will be your choice. For whatever you choose is foreknown by Him and Him alone, and we trust that He has accounted for it, for He has. So while I hope you choose to listen to me, just know that really, it is up to you. You benefit, after all. ...Alright, I've talked your ears off by now. Enjoy a bit of a rest; that's how all of man's labor should start. I will contact you again soon. Farewell, friend.'
A Story by the Fire
You feel the presence fade. Something tells you it should return eventually.
Thank you for listening'
Kill an enemy while blind firing

How are they gonna know which one I'm- well that worked.

You call out to the higher-pitched voice. You aren't sure how to make it clear which one you're calling out to, you just sort of... intend it towards that one. 'Hello', the voice says calmly, warmly. 'I was wondering when you would respond to me. You seem to be doing alright for yourself, in spite of the death you suffered earlier. ...Yes, I'm afraid to say that you *did*, in fact, perish. Yet, you are still here. ...I think we have a lot to talk about.'
The Higher-pitched Voice
(See next quest)
What... what just happened.

You can't decide. Or, perhaps, you want to choose both. Or neither. Either way, the voices suddenly grow deafeningly loud, like yelling. The temperature in the hideout spikes, the hairs on your body standing straight up. You feel as if a war is being waged right in front of you, though nothing is visibly happening. You feel a need to run, but you can't move either. Eternity passes, but no time has elapsed at all. Silence floods the hideout as quickly as it was broken. The voices dissapear.
Both Voices...?
***Be careful what quest you complete next.***
Continue
Continue
Which one...

'Hello???' You hear the higher pitched voice calling out to you, separated from the other and clear as the night sky. 'Hello, can you hear me? Do you understand me?' The voice seems almost desperate for you to answer them. It fades in and out, traded with the other voice. You get the sense that they are separated for good now, and that calling out to both won't work this time.
Connection

'Oh, thank goodness!' The voice seems rather relieved that you picked them. Or, do they even know about the choice you had? You'll have to ask another time, as the voice continues to rush on. 'Thank goodness. I was getting worried about... well, about many things. But...' You can hear the voice take a deep breath. It must be a living being of some sort, then. It continues. '...now, all is well. I apologize for being so flustered, I just... well, I have a lot to talk with you about.'
It seems you can only pick one...
Casually chatting with an otherwordly being

'I haven't even introduced myself... What a mess this has all been. Anyways. I am angel sent from Heaven. I am most often referred to as 'Guiding Light', though that's a story for another time. I've been here for a while now, in search of someone... that one might be you.' The voice pauses for a moment, as if waiting for you to confirm or deny the claim. '...well, the fact that you can communicate with me is enough proof for now. In that case...' The voice pauses again, though it seems to be busy with something. 'In that case, yes, I have a lot to tell you. Like, a LOT. Do you have the time for it?'
Conversation with an Otherwordly Being(2)
'A fire...? Ohhh, I see!' You feel the voice preparing for quite the speech.
Hand over any flammable material
***What will you do now?***
***Which voice do you call out to?***

'I can see you're rather reserved about this.' It's spoken like a statement, but you know it's asking you as well. 'I understand it, I do. Nothing makes sense right now. You're hearing voices, you've died and come back to life, nothing seems to change.' The voice speaks slowly, carefully, clearly trying to win you over. 'I can explain some of these things. Not everything, because there are some things I don't know either. But what I do know, I can tell you. ...if you're willing?'
Reluctant Cooperation

Why not, right? Why not listen to some voice explain what's going on? You decide to start a small fire outside your hideout, moreso to watch the flame than anything else. As it burns, however, you feel a presence join you. (See next quest)

I've noticed you have a spot for... other people to deliver you items? That's strange, are they maybe sent by the traders? Hmm... well, I guess we'll see what happens. Tell you what, I'll keep watch for you while you're out and about. I'll let you know what happens.
BONUS QUEST: Questionable Visitors

...just so you know, the cultists don't come and collect your offerings. It gets burnt up in a fire and replaced with something else. It's like something out of Exodus, it concerns me. The scavs are quite nice at least, they drop off random stuff, sometimes valuable too. They show up drunk sometimes, though... what's up with that?
Collect from the Scav case 15 times
Collect from the Cultists 15 times
I can do this, I know I can

You don't need some disembodied voice to help you. You're a PMC, dangit! You'll figure out this mess one way or another, like you always have. You're still here, you've made it this far... ...though, you did die earlier. But who cares, you're still here. You don't want to be here, but you're here, and you'll find a way out. You know you will.
Continue Your Search

No matter where you look, no matter what exit you take, you can't find a single thing that might help you. It doesn't seem like there's any way out of here, at least, any quick way. As you sit down to accept this fact, you hear a familiar voice.
Complete 5 raids

Stocks are an invaluable tool when it comes to assisting in recoil control. Instead of leaving it all to your hands, your shoulder and really your entire body can help absorb it. Let's take that away, shall we? You have to use an assault rifle, by the way. No cheating by using something that already has no stock!
Taking stock (literally)
Was that harder than you expected? I told you, stocks are really important.
Kill 25 enemies with an assault rifle without a stock
Wait... what's the last task going to be, if this is the fourth?
Remember how we talked about the two main ways to increase recoil control? You can't use either of them now. Still have to use an assault rifle, by the way,
Out of control
Hahahahaha... oh, you are so not ready for the next task. You shouldn't have completed this one, now you're in for it.
Kill 35 enemies with an assault rifle without a stock or foregrip
This... is hell...
Stocks. Foregrips. Both important, right? You poor soul. Use every single one. You have to kill a PMC as well, no picking on the scavs. Enjoy the suffering!
Suffer.

I can't believe you actually did this. Are you insane? What the heck. You must really, really care about recoil control. Well, good job. ...you want something for it? What, I'm an angel. Uhm... here, there's a little something in the corner of your shelter you never noticed. Go grab that.

'I must say, you've got quite an eye for things. You've found no shortage of things in this place, and most importantly, you've found yourself here to begin with. Where is here, you ask? Well, that depends on who you ask. To some, it's hell. To some, it's paradise. For me, well, I simply find myself here like you. Though I suppose I'm here in a different way than you. 'Both of us here without being sure why, both of us suddenly in contact with an otherworldly entity, both... ...what? You don't know? My friend, you are *definitely* a special being. 'Where do I begin... well, you know the tale of Tarkov better than most, since your company sent you here. You know about TerraGroup, you know the conflict, you probably know the result to some extent... but, you might not know much about the aftermath of these events. You see, ever since a, uh... 'special event' happened a few weeks ago, everything's been all messed up. Those who die come back to life. Those who could leave, don't. Tarkov still takes in new people, but doesn't seem to export any. I don't know what the outside world thinks is happening here, but there doesn't seem to be much intervention. Perhaps a space-time distortion of sorts? Who knows anymore... one the dead are raised, nothing's off the table. Anyways, I've been here watching ever since that 'event' transpired. I've been trying to understand what's going on, much like yourself. I haven't made much progress, unfortunatenly; there's only so much I can do by watching, and no one seems to hear me. Well, nobody but you. That works out great for me, I can conduct much better investigations with your help. Of course, that's if you're willing to help me. You can think about it, I'm in no rush. I'll be sure to make it worth your while. On that note, I have been talking for a while, haven't I. My apologies. Take the rest of the day off, you've had a wild introduction to this place. I'll be here if you need something.'
A story by the fire
You feel the presence fade. Something tells you it should return eventually.
Thank you for listening'

You- ...oh, you didn't know? That wasn't a hallucination, friend. You died, 100%. Pretty gnarly, too. Yet, you're still here. ...yeah, I should probably discuss some things with you.'

'Hello.' You hear the lower pitched voice calling out to you, separated from the other and clear as a sunny day. 'Hello, dear friend. Can you hear me alright?' The voice seems rather calm, though clearly seeking an answer. It fades in and out, traded with the other voice. You get the sense that they are separated for good now, and that calling out to both won't work this time.
Association

'Oh, good. I wasn't sure if I was able to speak with you or not.' The voice remains rather calm, almost as if they were sure you would pick them. Did they even know there was a choice? 'Sit down, make yourself comfortable. You have questions, and I have answers.'

'I should probably take a minute to actually explain who I am, shouldn't I? Well, I am actually an angel sent from Heaven. Yes, the big place in the sky, straight from God Himself. Do not be afraid, I have not come to judge you. I've actually come to work with you. Yes, *you*. One could say you're the `main character` of this place. As to why that is... well, that's a whole other story. You might as well get comfortable if I'm gonna keep rambling.'
Conversation with an otherwordly being(3)

Seriously? I HAVE to put up with one of you? Fuck me, I guess. At least it's not both of you.

Almost as soon as you decide to go without that weird voice, you hear it speak again. 'Perhaps you'd prefer to work with me?' It's... not the same voice, you don't think. This one is a bit deeper, its way of speaking a bit different. You hear it speak again. 'I, uh... I'm afraid you must choose one of us. Call it the curse of this place, if you will. It won't leave you alone unless you let it control you, and the only other option is to let me protect you from it. Which, yes, will mean I'm in contact with you instead. I'll be far less annoying, I promise.' As much as you'd rather listen to neither of these voices, you conclude there truly is no other option. You're running around enough for the traders, why not throw disembodied voices into the mix? Maybe they can reward you in some way. If nothing else, maybe working with them will shut them up some.
***Never truly alone***
***Your choice is made***
